teamObjective.explanation: In the endgame race, retaining the lead is critical. Leading the strongest single maximizes the probability that I win the trick and get to play my final card.

partnerBelief.summary: My partner (player 2) holds 3 unknown cards and has not yet acted. Because I am about to finish (2 cards), my partner likely wants me to retain or regain the lead so I can shed my last card, or alternatively take control themselves if they can finish first. With no public signals, I cannot assume partner has a specific card, only that they have 3 random-ish unknown cards that may include trumps or higher singles.

opponentBelief.summary: Each opponent holds 3 unknown cards. They want to prevent me from going out, so if I lead a low single they are likely to beat it if they can. They may hold trump 7s or red trumps that beat any non-trump single, but I cannot assert that as a fact.

actionRationale.primaryReason: In the endgame race, retaining the lead is critical. Leading the strongest single maximizes the probability that I win the trick and get to play my final card.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Legal alternative retained for verifier comparison after schema repair.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.

notes: Deterministically schema-repaired from a ToM-prompted raw output; selectedActionId is unchanged.
